behaviors.description: Required patient fields are validated up front, and missing fields return one clear error listing all missing items.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is local validation logic: compute missing keys and format one error. Rejected Integration because the behavior text is about field checks/error content, not a required cross-module handoff; downstream suppression can be asserted by a local collaborator spy.
behaviors.test_description: Call the validator/intake-entry function with partial payloads (missing one, two, all required fields) and assert returned error lists exactly all missing fields and that downstream steps are not invoked.

behaviors.description: DOB validation rejects future dates, dates older than 150 years, and non-date strings.
behaviors.justification: Pure rule checking over one input value; no module wiring or I/O is needed to expose real failures. Rejected Integration/System because behavior text specifies only date parsing/range logic.
behaviors.test_description: Test DOB validator with boundary dates (today, tomorrow, exactly 150 years ago, 150 years + 1 day) and malformed strings; assert valid/invalid outcomes and error reasons.

behaviors.description: Insurance eligibility call sends correct patient ID/plan code and interprets eligible/not eligible/pending.
behaviors.justification: This is an external API contract behavior (request shape + real HTTP response mapping). MOCK-HIDE risk is high: in-process stubs can miss real serialization/header/status quirks seen over HTTP. Rejected Integration because behavior text explicitly requires correct call to the payer API.
behaviors.test_description: Run against a real HTTP test server (or payer sandbox) and assert outbound payload contains exact patient ID/plan code; return each status variant and verify mapped internal eligibility state.

behaviors.description: If payer API is unreachable, eligibility becomes pending and intake continues.
behaviors.justification: Failure depends on real network/subprocess error modes (timeout, connection refused, DNS). Mocked exceptions often under-represent production failure shapes, so System is the minimum reliable boundary.
behaviors.test_description: Point payer client to an unreachable endpoint (or blackholed host), run intake, assert eligibility is set to pending and pipeline advances to later stages instead of aborting.

behaviors.description: Triage invocation payload contains reported symptoms and configured emergency rubric.
behaviors.justification: This verifies module handoff/wiring: intake data + config must be combined and passed to model client. Rejected System/Agentic because behavior text is payload composition, not real model output quality.
behaviors.test_description: Execute triage service with fixture symptoms and rubric config using a stub model endpoint; assert outbound model request includes both exact symptom text and rubric content.

behaviors.description: Live AI triage assigns high urgency for emergency symptom combinations (e.g., chest pain + shortness of breath).
behaviors.justification: Correctness depends on real model judgment quality and non-deterministic output distribution. Deterministic stubs cannot reveal misclassification failures.
behaviors.test_description: Run repeated live triage calls on emergency-case fixtures and assert high-urgency classification rate meets threshold; track variance and fail if confidence drops below accepted floor.

behaviors.description: Triage parser persists a clinician-readable explanation field, not just numeric score.
behaviors.justification: This is a local handoff/persistence behavior: parser output must map into storage schema correctly. Rejected Agentic because behavior text is storage of explanation field presence/content pass-through, not evaluating model reasoning quality.
behaviors.test_description: Feed triage response fixture containing score + explanation through parse-and-save path, then read stored record and assert explanation field exists and is non-empty alongside score.

behaviors.description: Live AI triage explanation is case-specific and gives urgency rationale, not generic boilerplate.
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly evaluates generated explanation quality and relevance to symptoms, which only appears with real model outputs. Mock outputs would trivially pass and hide true failures.
behaviors.test_description: Use live model on multiple symptom cases; score explanation for symptom mention coverage and rationale specificity (heuristic rubric/human-labeled set) and fail when generic-template rate exceeds threshold.

behaviors.description: Scheduling picks first available slot matching urgency SLA (high same-day, medium within 48h).
behaviors.justification: Requires scheduler logic plus calendar DB state interaction, but can be validated fully in-process with local DB fixtures. Rejected System because no real external network/OS contract is required by this behavior text.
behaviors.test_description: Seed calendar DB with representative availability, run scheduler for high/medium urgency patients, assert selected slot is earliest one satisfying same-day/48h rule.

behaviors.description: Scheduling prevents double-booking under concurrent patient processing.
behaviors.justification: Real failure is concurrent state handoff/transaction behavior in shared local DB; this exceeds Unit. Rejected System because behavior can be reproduced with in-process concurrency + real local DB locking semantics, without external OS/network contracts.
behaviors.test_description: Start two concurrent scheduling attempts for same candidate slot against test DB with production-like constraints/transactions; assert only one booking commits and the other is retried/reassigned/fails cleanly.

behaviors.description: Confirmation includes patient name, appointment time, provider name, and visit-type-specific preparation instructions.
behaviors.justification: Template/rendering rules are local deterministic composition logic. Rejected Integration because behavior text does not require external delivery channel behavior, only message content.
behaviors.test_description: Render confirmation for each visit type with fixture appointment/patient/provider data and assert all required fields plus correct prep instructions are present.

behaviors.description: Deterministic pipeline orchestration (validate → insurance → triage stub → schedule → confirm) completes correctly.
behaviors.justification: This is cross-module orchestration/state propagation with deterministic triage stub, so real model quality is explicitly out of scope. Rejected Agentic by behavior text: "deterministic stubbed triage output" disqualifies dependence on real AI output.
behaviors.test_description: Run end-to-end in-process pipeline with stubbed insurance/triage responses and test DB, assert stage order, persisted state transitions, and final confirmation emission.

behaviors.description: Full live intake pipeline completes for a real patient submission without manual intervention.
behaviors.justification: Includes live AI triage, so correctness depends on real model behavior in a production-like run; mocks would hide failures tied to model outputs. Rejected Workflow because behavior text includes one agentic stage (triage) within the pipeline, not multiple sequenced agentic decisions.
behaviors.test_description: Execute one fully live intake submission through validate, real payer call, live triage, scheduling, and confirmation; assert successful terminal state and no human override path triggered.
